issues.title: Consolidate google vertex starters into one starter
issues.body: There are separate boot starters for chat and embedding.  To be consistent in the starters across the project, these two starters should be merged into a single boot starter for google vertex.

comments.body:
from

```
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-ai-vertex-ai-embedding-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
</dependency>
```
and
```
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-ai-vertex-ai-gemini-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
</dependency>
```
to 
```
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-ai-vertex-ai-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
</dependency>
```

which would autoconfigure both the embedding and chat models.  We can deprecate the current starters in M2 and remove them in RC.
Closing this for now and revisit if there is a significant interest in getting a single starter for both embedding/chat models.

issues.title: How can I specify AWS profile from the configuration file? Bedrock client
issues.body:
With `aws.sdk.kotlin:bedrockruntime` I can setup the client like this:

```kts
val client = BedrockRuntimeClient {
        region = "eu-central-1"
        credentialsProvider = ProfileCredentialsProvider(profileName = "dev")
    }
```

where `profileName` delegates to one of the profiles in `~/.aws/config`, i.e. `[profile dev]`

It's it possible to do now with Spring AI? If not can we have the feature request?

comments.body:
According to [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/689e458648d50a2de7ca571f5ba2e66a3b055e75/spring-ai-spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/autoconfigure/bedrock/BedrockAwsConnectionConfiguration.java#L42) it is not specified in the bedrock properties. 
An option would be to add the logic if there is a profile name to extend it. 
Otherwise you could also just set the `AWS_PROFILE` environment variable and it should pick it up.
We are no longer supporting the bedrock api to access chat models, have switched to using bedrock converse apis

issues.title: How to Integrate RedisVectorStore with RedissonConnectionFactory
issues.body: Please do a quick search on GitHub issues first, the feature you are about to request might have already been requested. **Expected Behavior** I hope to be able to use RedisVectorStore with other RedisConnectionFactory implementations as well. **Current Behavior** I have noticed that the current instantiation of RedisVectorStore uses JedisConnectionFactory from the container. However, my project uses RedissonConnectionFactory, which cannot retrieve the required parameters for the instantiation of RedisVectorStore.
